dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7258 EH: Energy Emergency Leadership Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.official-title: To amend the Department of Energy Organization Act with respect to functions assigned to Assistant Secretaries, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Energy Emergency Leadership Act.

subsection.text: Subsection (a) of section 203 of the Department of Energy Organization Act (42 U.S.C. 7133(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: Energy emergency and energy security functions, including—

subparagraph.text: responsibilities with respect to energy infrastructure, security and resilience, emerging threats, cybersecurity, supply, and emergency planning and preparedness, coordination, response, and restoration, as appropriate; and

upon request of a State, local, or Tribal government or energy sector entity, and in coordination with other Federal agencies as appropriate, provision of technical assistance and support to protect against, detect, and respond to energy security threats, risks, and incidents.

subsection.text: The Secretary of Energy shall ensure that the functions of the Secretary described in section 203(a)(12) of the Department of Energy Organization Act (as added by this Act) are performed in coordination with relevant Federal agencies.

attestation-group.attestation-date: Passed the House of Representatives June 29, 2026.
